description: Skill has no output format section; steps describe what tools to run but do not specify what the final debugging report should contain or how to structure it
suggested_fix: Add an Output Format section specifying: root cause summary, affected code paths, recommended fix, and confidence level

description: "related" is a vague quantifier: 'find code related to the issue' does not constrain the search scope
suggested_fix: Replace with specific criteria, e.g., 'find functions by name or keyword matching the error message or module name'

description: Skill has no output format section; no guidance on what the exploration summary should contain or how to present findings
suggested_fix: Add an Output Format section: architecture summary, community list, key entry points, and suggested next exploration paths

description: "complex" is a vague quantifier in Tips: 'identify complex code' gives no measurable threshold
suggested_fix: Replace with a measurable criterion, e.g., 'functions over 50 lines or with more than 10 branches'

description: Skill has no output format section; no template for the refactoring plan or safety report
suggested_fix: Add an Output Format section: list of proposed changes, impact assessment per change, and rollback plan

description: "major" is a vague quantifier in Safety Checks: 'before major refactors' sets no actionable threshold
suggested_fix: Replace with a measurable threshold, e.g., 'before refactors affecting more than 5 callers or crossing module boundaries'

description: "significant" is a vague quantifier in step 5: 'files with significant changes' does not define what qualifies
suggested_fix: Replace with a measurable criterion, e.g., 'files with more than 20 changed lines or public API modifications'

description: "large" is a vague quantifier in Tips: 'For large PRs' sets no actionable threshold
suggested_fix: Replace with a measurable threshold, e.g., 'For PRs touching more than 10 files'

description: "related" is a vague quantifier in Tips: 'find related code the PR might have missed'
suggested_fix: Replace with specific search intent, e.g., 'find callers of modified public APIs or sibling modules in the same community'

description: "many" is a vague quantifier in step 3: 'Files with many dependents (high-risk changes)' — no threshold defined
suggested_fix: Replace with a measurable threshold, e.g., 'Files with more than 5 direct dependents'

description: "significant" is a vague quantifier: 'This saves significant tokens by avoiding full codebase scans'
suggested_fix: Quantify the savings, e.g., 'saves 5-10x tokens compared to manual file scanning'

description: Notes section lists 14 supported languages but CLAUDE.md architecture documents 19, explicitly naming Dart, R, Perl, Lua, and Jupyter/Databricks notebooks as additional languages
suggested_fix: Update the language list in Notes to match CLAUDE.md: 19 languages including Vue SFC, Solidity, Dart, R, Perl, Lua, and Jupyter/Databricks notebooks

description: uvx invokes 'code-review-graph' without a version pin; each session fetches and runs the latest published PyPI release, so a compromised or broken release is auto-adopted
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version: change args to ['code-review-graph==<current-version>', 'serve'] and update after intentional releases

description: Makes outbound HTTP requests to third-party APIs (MiniMax, Google Gemini, OpenAI-compatible endpoints) carrying API keys from environment variables; code content is included in request bodies when embeddings are computed
suggested_fix: Document exactly which data is sent to each provider; enforce TLS verification; ensure error messages do not echo sensitive source content

description: npm dependencies use caret ranges (better-sqlite3 ^12.4.1, d3 ^7.9.0, esbuild ^0.20.0) without a committed lockfile, allowing unexpected minor/patch upgrades on install
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions with --save-exact or commit package-lock.json to the repository

description: review-delta and review-pr use the '_tool' suffix for MCP tool names (get_review_context_tool, query_graph_tool, build_or_update_graph_tool) while debug-issue, explore-codebase, and review-changes use bare names; CLAUDE.md Key Tools table also uses bare names
suggested_fix: Standardize all skill files to bare tool names (without '_tool' suffix) to match CLAUDE.md and the majority pattern

description: build-graph Notes lists 14 languages; CLAUDE.md documents 19 — the skill's language list has diverged from the canonical architecture documentation
suggested_fix: Sync the language list with CLAUDE.md: add Dart, R, Perl, Lua, and Jupyter/Databricks notebooks to the Notes section
